title: Post-workout Ukrainian Chewy Nigiri
description: A winey and chewy post-workout dish inspired by Ukrainian cuisine, featuring low-fat-friendly ingredients and nigiri-shaping technique.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine buckwheat groats, potato starch, and water to form a sticky dough.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 6-8 equal pieces and shape each piece into a small rectangle.
Place a small amount of olive oil on each rectangle and sprinkle with feta cheese, diced eggs, chopped dill, and a pinch of salt and black pepper.
Using a sushi mat or a piece of parchment paper, shape each rectangle into a nigiri shape, applying gentle pressure.
Repeat with the remaining dough pieces.
Place the nigiri on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and let them rise in a warm place for 30-45 minutes, or until slightly puffed.
Bake the nigiri for 12-15 minutes, or until lightly golden brown.
Meanwhile, prepare the wine reduction by combining rice vinegar, sugar, and 2 tbsp of olive oil in a small saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a simmer over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the sugar dissolves.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
Brush the wine reduction on the baked nigiri and serve warm.
Serve immediately, garnished with additional fresh dill if desired.
